msgs.text: I'll look through the project memory files related to the gq2-lean work, and also check whether the gq2-lean project has its own memory directory.
That's the record. Let me also check the wave commit messages, which the ledger says contain the detailed wave reports.

Found them. The decisions aren't in the memory directory — the formalization memory ([gq2-lean-formalization.md](../../.claude/projects/-Users-roed-claude-lmfdb/memory/gq2-lean-formalization.md)) ends on Jul 9 with the B13 flip, before the cleanup started. The cleanup orchestrator instead keeps its ledger inside the repo at [.mathlib-quality/cleanup-progress.md](../gq2-lean/.mathlib-quality/cleanup-progress.md) (untracked, "orchestration state — not committed"), with detailed per-wave reports in the git commit messages. Here's what it records:

**The token/timeout decisions** — the ledger has a "PROTOCOL THAT WORKS" section that directly addresses the problems you mention:

- **Workers do in-context golf only** — no sub-agent spawning and no background waiting, with the explicit note that *"waiting" turns terminate agents*. That was the timeout fix.
- **Workers never run full `lake build`** (the big token/time sink); they check their own file with `lake env lean <file>` plus `lean_diagnostic_messages`. The orchestrator runs the full `lake build` + `check_axioms.sh` gate between waves and commits each green wave with pathspec staging.
- **Buzz (performance) work is timeboxed to 2 attempts per declaration**, and workers may never raise `maxHeartbeats`.
- Waves of **≤6 workers on disjoint files**; no statement/signature/docstring changes; renames are only appended to `renames.jsonl`, never applied.
- **Model tiering**: Fable for ≥1500-line / design-sensitive files plus the 4 review-cone files (Words, Statement, SectionTenSources, Reconstruction — saved for last with strictest statement preservation); Opus for everything else.

**The latest recorded state is a pause, not a run.** As of ~03:10 this morning the ledger's top entry says: *PAUSED 2026-07-11 — Fable 5 usage credits exhausted (account-level)*. Both Fable-tier workers in wave 7 (Shapiro/Ledger and Phase140/GammaA) died mid-edit; their unverified partial edits were **reverted to baseline**, while the Opus file (AnabelianBridge, −3) completed, gated green, and was committed as "wave 7 (partial)" (6d2e1ad). Recorded resume options: (a) top up Fable credits and continue as planned, or (b) reassign the remaining Fable-tier files to Opus. So if an agent is mid-run now, it resumed after that pause — the ledger hasn't been updated since.

**Progress so far**: 24/170 files cleaned across waves 1–7, net ≈ −849 lines, all waves gated green (census 9, zero sorries). Several stale `maxHeartbeats` raises were retired (SectionSeven, GaussZ/FinalGammaA ×3, GaussCount lowered to 800k); the two reverted Fable files still carry theirs, including the likely-genuine `synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 4000000` bottleneck in Phase140/GammaA. Deferred queues are also recorded: 5 pending renames in `renames.jsonl` (one flagged dead pending your delete-vs-rename decision on `SectionNine.isScalarStack.normal`), a cross-file helper list (e.g. `ElemDual.add_self` hand-rolled in 10 places), a `/decompose-proof` shortlist headed by `wall_count_aux`, and `/split-file` candidates (FoxHeisenberg, SectionSeven, RegularSummand).

If it'd be useful, I can append a short pointer entry to the gq2 memory file so future sessions know the cleanup ledger lives in `.mathlib-quality/` rather than in memory — just say the word.
I'll resume the cleanup marathon as orchestrator. First, let me load the cleanup-all skill to follow the established protocol exactly.
Clean state confirmed: master in sync with origin, no other agent active, tree clean, 03:34 now (target wrap ~08:30). Next: build the remaining-file queue and run the baseline gate.
**Queue: 140 files / 54,460 lines remaining** (+4 review-cone files reserved for last). Running the baseline gate before dispatching.
`lake` isn't on this shell's PATH — locating the toolchain and re-running the build gate.
**54,460 lines / 140 files remaining. Wave 8 dispatched (6 workers: 3 Fable + 3 Opus, 8,722 lines in flight).** Waiting on worker reports; next update at the wave-8 gate.
**Wave 8: 1/6 reported (Phase140/Local green, −9, stacked 4M/1.6M raises retired). 5 in flight, backstop 09:10.**
**Wave 8: 2/6 reported (Prop32 green, −38, both 1M raises retired at default heartbeats). 4 in flight, backstop 09:10.**
WordCoh2's Fable worker stalled mid-edit (watchdog kill — same failure mode as the previous run). Attempting one resume to have it finish and verify; otherwise it gets reverted at the 09:10 backstop.
**Wave 8: 2/6 landed, 4 resumed with finish-up-only directives. Backstop 09:10.**
**Wave 8: 3/6 landed (KappaNormalForm green, −22). 3 Fable resumes in flight, backstop 09:10.**
**Wave 8: 4/6 landed (Shapiro/Ledger green, −109, 1M raise retired, 9 deprecation warnings cleared). 2 in flight, backstop 09:10.**
**Wave 8: 5/6 landed (WordCoh2 green, −94). Phase140/GammaA in flight, backstop 09:10.**
**Wave 8 complete: 6/6 green, net −434 lines, heartbeat raises retired in 4 files (incl. the 4M synthInstance lowered to 2M).** Running the wave gate now.
Tree is exactly the six wave files (−434 net). Launching the gate build in the background.
**Wave-8 gate running (full build + census).** Commit/push and wrap-up follow on green.
Gate green (8756 jobs, census 9, zero sorries). Committing and pushing wave 8.
Pushed as 9ec72df. Updating the orchestration ledger and memory, then the wrap-up report.

## Overnight report

**Wave 8 landed, gated green, and pushed (9ec72df): 6 files cleaned, net −434 lines, and stale `maxHeartbeats` raises retired in 4 of them — including both files that killed the previous run.** Only one wave fit in the window, for reasons below, but it was the biggest wave of the marathon so far. The repo is at a clean pause point: tree clean, `master` == `origin/master`, `lake build` green (8756 jobs), census 9, zero sorries.

| File | Δ lines | Heartbeat raises |
|---|---|---|
| Shapiro/Ledger | 1720 → 1611 (−109) | 1M **removed**; 9 deprecation warnings cleared |
| Phase140/GammaA | 1626 → 1464 (−162) | stale 1.6M **removed**; genuine synthInstance **lowered 4M → 2M** |
| WordCoh2 | 1522 → 1428 (−94) | none present |
| Phase140/Local | 1296 → 1287 (−9) | **all four** stacked raise lines removed (stale >8×/>20×) |
| Prop32 | 1280 → 1242 (−38) | both 1M **removed** |
| KappaNormalForm | 1278 → 1256 (−22) | none present |

All six worker reports confirmed statements/signatures/docstrings byte-identical via git-diff review; renames queued: 0.

**Why only one wave.** Two compounding causes, both now recorded in the ledger for future runs. First, six concurrent workers each running `lake env lean` contend heavily on this machine — the wave ran ~5 hours wall versus the ~45 minutes your previous 3-worker waves took, so 3–4 workers per wave is the right calibration going forward. Second, around 08:30 a platform-side API stream stall hit **all four** still-running workers at once (600-second watchdog kill — not credit exhaustion; Fable credits held fine all night, ~1.1M subagent tokens total). The good news: resuming each stalled worker in place with a "finish up, verify, no more golfing" message worked 4-for-4 — no work was lost and nothing had to be reverted, unlike the previous run.

**Marathon state: 31/170 files done, net ≈ −1286 lines.** The remaining queue is now entirely Opus-tier by size — next up: RStage/GammaA (1181), CentralObstruction (1112), OrbitDecomp (1056), SectionSix (1051), LocalKummer (1025), HilbertLedger (901, raise-pending) — with Zhat and the 4 review-cone files held back for Fable last, per protocol.

Records: the ledger ([.mathlib-quality/cleanup-progress.md](../gq2-lean/.mathlib-quality/cleanup-progress.md)) is fully updated — wave-8 log, the two operational lessons, the refreshed queue, and a grown /decompose-proof shortlist (wave 8 surfaced ~10 more >100-line proofs, `hpartial_local` ~475 the largest). I also added the memory pointer I'd offered: [gq2-cleanup-marathon.md](../../.claude/projects/-Users-roed-claude-lmfdb/memory/gq2-cleanup-marathon.md), so future sessions know the ledger lives in the repo and that stalled workers should be resumed, not reverted.

To continue the marathon, just say the word — the next wave dispatches straight from the ledger queue (I'd run it at 3–4 workers this time).

**Recommendation: wait until you land.** Don't start a wave now — here's the arithmetic:

- Even a small 3-worker wave needs ~45–60 minutes of connected time to reach the gate (workers finish → full `lake build` + census → commit). In 20 minutes, workers would be mid-edit when your laptop drops off the network, and everything — orchestrator included — dies with the connection. You'd land to a dirty tree of unverified partial edits on tracked files, the exact triage mess the ledger warns about.
- The repo is currently at a *perfect* pause point: green, committed, pushed, ledger current. Waiting costs nothing; resuming is one message.

Your explanation also corrects my overnight diagnosis, which matters more than the scheduling question: the 5-hour stall wasn't compute contention or an API failure — the workers were **blocked on file-access approval prompts** that nobody was awake to approve (gq2-lean sits outside this session's working directory, so worker edits there needed interactive permission). Let me fix the two records I wrote, since they attribute the delay to the wrong cause:

Both records are corrected (ledger + memory) — the real lesson is now recorded: *pre-approve file access to the gq2-lean tree before any unattended run*.

One thing worth doing in your remaining ~15 minutes, if you're up for it: I can add a permission rule to this project's settings so workers editing `[local path]` never prompt again. That's the single change that makes the next overnight run actually run unattended — otherwise the same stall will recur the next time nobody's watching the prompts. Say "yes" and I'll set it up now (takes under a minute); then when you land, one message ("run the next wave") resumes the marathon at 3–4 workers with nothing to approve.

Otherwise: safe travels — everything is committed and pushed, and the marathon resumes cleanly whenever you're back online.
Valid — all 36 existing rules preserved, new key in place. Marking it done in the ledger and memory so the next session knows it's already configured:

Done — and no workers started, as requested.

**What changed:** [.claude/settings.local.json](.claude/settings.local.json) now has `"additionalDirectories": ["[local path]"]` under `permissions`. Your existing rules already allowed `Edit`/`Write`/`Bash` tool-wide with `acceptEdits` mode, but those only apply *inside the session's directory scope* — and gq2-lean sat outside it, which is why every worker edit over there queued an approval prompt overnight. Adding the directory to the scope closes exactly that gap; all 36 of your existing allow rules are untouched (JSON validated with `jq`).

**Caveats, briefly:** this is the local (gitignored-by-convention, machine-specific) settings file, so nothing lands in the repo. The change hot-reloads in most cases since the file already existed — but if the *first* worker of the next wave still prompts, a session restart will definitely pick it up; worth glancing at the first dispatch for a minute before walking away. Both the marathon ledger and the memory note now record that this is configured, so the next orchestrator won't re-litigate it.

**State you're leaving at:** wave 8 committed and pushed (9ec72df), tree clean, build green, census 9. When you land, one message — "run the next wave" — resumes from the ledger queue (RStage/GammaA, CentralObstruction, OrbitDecomp, SectionSix, LocalKummer, HilbertLedger are next, all Opus-tier). Safe flight! ✈️
Wave 9 going out: the next six files from the ledger queue, all Opus-tier, six workers as requested. Quick baseline sanity first, then dispatch.

**Wave 9 dispatched: 6 Opus workers (RStage/GammaA 1181, CentralObstruction 1112, OrbitDecomp 1056, SectionSix 1051, LocalKummer 1025, HilbertLedger 901 — 6,326 lines in flight). Backstop at ~17:06.** With the permissions fix in place, workers should run prompt-free this time. Gate → commit → push on completion; next report at the wave-9 gate.

The gate command went out without an explicit `cd` — background shells can start at the project root, which has no lakefile. Relaunching it properly:
That relaunch still omitted the `cd` — fixing it for real this time:

The gate is genuinely running — lake found the lakefile (the shell's working directory persisted at gq2-lean), and the piped `tail` only prints at completion. My two kill-and-relaunch cycles were unnecessary caution; the current run (blggiveo9) is the live gate. **Waiting on the wave-9 gate; commit + push + report on green.**
Gate green (8756 jobs, census 9, zero sorries). Committing and pushing wave 9.

## Wave 9 report

**Landed, gated green, and pushed (a396ef7): 6 files, net −167 lines, in ~55 minutes end-to-end.** The permissions fix did its job — all six Opus workers ran prompt-free and finished in 30–40 minutes each, versus 5 hours for wave 8. Six-worker waves are confirmed viable now that approvals aren't in the loop.

| File | Δ lines | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| RStage/GammaA | 1181 → 1132 (−49) | ~15 rename-indirection `have`s inlined |
| OrbitDecomp | 1056 → 1012 (−44) | `Finset.sum_apply` replaced two hand-rolled inductions |
| CentralObstruction | 1112 → 1081 (−31) | `Commute.pow_left` killed a 3×-repeated helper |
| LocalKummer | 1025 → 1006 (−19) | `add_eq_left` cancellations, subgoal folds |
| SectionSix | 1051 → 1038 (−13) | statement-anchor file; extra-strict diff review, 1 warning cleared |
| HilbertLedger | 901 → 890 (−11) | see below |

One genuinely useful negative result: **HilbertLedger's `maxHeartbeats 2400000` is real, not stale.** The worker's two-attempt floor search (removal, then 600k) both hit `whnf` timeouts on `evensNorm_deepUnit_vanish` (~180-line Tier-4 assembly), so the raise is kept with that justification and moved off the pending list. Retiring it will take a `/decompose-proof` pass on that theorem, which I've queued in the ledger's deferred list along with seven other >70-line proofs wave 9 surfaced, plus two near-duplicate proof blocks worth extracting later.

**Marathon state: 37/170 files, net ≈ −1453 lines.** Gate was clean as always: `lake build` green (8756 jobs), census 9, zero sorries. Ledger and memory are updated; tree is clean and `master` matches origin.

I've paused here since you asked for one wave — the queue continues with AffineTLift (882), GaussZ/GammaAD (857), GaussSigns (813), VLiftCount (799), RStage/ObstructionBuild (714), and OrbitVanish (706), all Opus-tier. At wave-9 pace that's ~1 hour per 6-file wave, ~6k lines of coverage each. Whenever you're back online: "keep running waves" and I'll chain them with gates in between.

## 🏁 The per-file cleanup marathon is complete

**All 170 files in the library have now been processed (waves 1–22), for a cumulative net reduction of ≈ 2,719 lines (~86.9k → ~84.2k), with every wave gated green and pushed — `master` is at 67ff3c6, working tree clean, `lake build` green (8756 jobs), axiom census 9, zero sorries, no `native_decide`.**

Since your "keep running waves" this afternoon, waves 9–22 ran continuously for ~10 hours: 133 files across 14 waves, scaling from one file per worker down through pairs, triples, and quads as the files got smaller, and finishing with a Fable-tier finale on the review-cone and main-theorem files. Every worker obeyed bodies-only rules — all statements, signatures, docstrings, and attributes are byte-identical repo-wide, verified per-file by git-diff inspection and re-verified by the full build at each of the 14 gates.

**Highlights of the run:**

- **Heartbeat raises are fully resolved.** Every stale `maxHeartbeats` raise in the repo was retired (most proved stale by 4–32×). Exactly three remain, each documented: HilbertLedger's 2.4M (floor-search proved genuine), Phase140/GammaA's synthInstance 2M (genuine, already halved from 4M), and GaussCount's 800k (retires when `wall_count_aux` gets decomposed).
- **The paper contract surface is certified.** `PresentationLiteral`, `Statement`, `Words`, and `Foundations` were audited and left byte-identical; `Reconstruction` (−28) and `Zhat` (−18) got genuine mathlib substitutions with load-bearing instances explicitly probe-tested and preserved.
- **Best single finds:** a 90-line verbatim duplicate re-derivation in Half139Local replaced by a call to the file's own theorem (−77, and the file compiles faster); DyadicNielsen's six simp-lemma bodies collapsed to defeq (−49); `Finite.card_subtype_lt`, `QuotientGroup.discreteTopology`, `Odd.of_dvd_nat` and ~100 other mathlib substitutions replacing hand-rolled proofs.
- **Process failures were all recovered without losing work:** the wave-14 gate caught the one bad edit that slipped a worker's verification (an out-of-closure lemma from the known `lean_multi_attempt` false-positive trap — prompts and gates were hardened after it), and several mid-wave stalls/drops were resumed in place.

**What remains is deliberately not done, because it needs your sign-off** — it's all statement- or structure-touching, catalogued in [.mathlib-quality/cleanup-progress.md](../gq2-lean/.mathlib-quality/cleanup-progress.md):

**[.mathlib-quality/DEFERRED-PASSES-HANDOFF.md](../gq2-lean/.mathlib-quality/DEFERRED-PASSES-HANDOFF.md)** is a self-contained resume doc for Phase D-ii + Phase E. To pick up in a fresh session, just say:

> *Read `.mathlib-quality/DEFERRED-PASSES-HANDOFF.md` and continue the deferred-pass work.*

It contains the current state + verification commands, the exact gate protocol (including the pipefail and background-build gotchas), the **hard-won D-i orchestration lessons** (never parallelize import-dependent decompose lanes; the anti-destruction worker clause; streaming-builds-only; Opus over Fable for this work), a ready worker-prompt template, the D-ii target list pre-grouped into import-independent batches with current file sizes, the serial Phase-E split plan, and the reconciled list of open flags. The ledger and project memory both point at it.

## State you're leaving at

Repo is at a clean, fully-committed milestone: **`master` @ 0195c35, in sync with origin, working tree clean, build green (8756 jobs), census 9, zero sorries.**

What the deferred passes accomplished today, on top of the 170-file marathon:
- **A** — rename queue drained (7 renames incl. `MinimalBlock.R → frattiniK` across 21 files, + 1 dead-decl deletion)
- **B** — 6 owner-flag fixes, 2 standing warnings eliminated
- **C** — cross-file dedup (−281 lines; `finConsAddEquiv` turned out to be mathlib's `Fin.consLinearEquiv`)
- **D-i** — the **12 heaviest proofs decomposed**, and the headline: **every heartbeat raise in the repo retired or minimized** — 800k gone entirely, 2.4M → 300k, 2M → 800k

## Remaining (documented, not started)

- **D-ii** — ~15 more long proofs; the explicitly *lower-value* stretch (readability only, no perf wins)
- **E** — split FoxHeisenberg (2955) and SectionSeven (2705); real structural value, high-risk, serial

Both are fully scoped in the handoff. The one thing I'd re-flag for whenever you resume: D-i proved that parallel decompose lanes on this shared, reset-prone tree fight each other, so the remaining work is genuinely slower per unit — worth doing in small committed batches rather than a big fan-out. Have a good night.
